name: Self-interacting dark matter with Yukawa dark force
summary: Dark matter with a light mediator phi (mass m_phi << m_chi) self-scatters through a Yukawa potential V(r) = -alpha_chi exp(-m_phi r) / r. The resulting cross-section is highly velocity-dependent: rises at low v (Sommerfeld-enhanced regime), peaks at v ~ m_phi/m_chi, then falls. Naturally produces the right effective cross-section sigma/m ~ 1 cm^2/g on dwarf-galaxy scales (v ~ 30 km/s, where cores are observed) while satisfying sigma/m < 0.1 cm^2/g on cluster scales (v ~ 1500 km/s, where halos are cuspy). Single framework explains the rotation-curve-diversity problem and the cluster-vs-dwarf core-cusp transition.

addresses_puzzles.note: Kaplinghat-Tulin-Yu-Zurek framework with m_chi ~ 100 GeV, m_phi ~ 10 MeV, alpha_chi ~ 10^-2 fits the full diversity of dwarf-galaxy rotation curves while respecting Bullet-Cluster bound (Kamada et al. 2017, arXiv:1611.02716). Predicts characteristic core-radius / V_max scaling that LSST + JWST + ALMA can test.

Resolves both the core-cusp problem (Yukawa potential softens central density) and too-big-to-fail (cores reduce inner circular velocities). Missing satellites partly addressed; full resolution still requires baryonic physics.

DM thermal-relic in dark sector with dark photon/scalar mediator; relic abundance set by Sommerfeld-enhanced chi chi -> phi phi annihilation. Consistent with direct-detection limits via small Higgs/photon kinetic-mixing portal.

touches_benchmarks.note: Sommerfeld-enhanced annihilation in dwarfs gives boosted indirect-detection signal; constrains alpha_chi and m_phi via Fermi-LAT dwarf spheroidal observations.

new_symmetries: hidden U(1) or Z_2 stabilising chi
introduces.note: DM mass, mediator mass, dark fine-structure constant alpha_chi.

history: Original SIDM proposal: Spergel-Steinhardt (2000), PRL 84, 3760. Yukawa-mediated SIDM: Buckley-Fox (2010), PRD 81, 083522, arXiv:0911.3898. Tulin-Yu-Zurek (2013), 'Beyond collisionless dark matter: particle physics dynamics for dark matter halo structure', PRD 87, 115007, arXiv:1302.3898 - velocity-dependent framework. Halo-diversity application: Kamada-Kaplinghat-Pace-Yu (2017, arXiv:1611.02716).

references.citation: Tulin, Yu, Zurek, 'Beyond collisionless dark matter: particle physics dynamics for dark matter halo structure', Phys. Rev. D 87, 115007 (2013)
Kamada, Kaplinghat, Pace, Yu, 'Self-interacting dark matter can explain diverse galactic rotation curves', Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 111102 (2017)
